folio.label: 41r
folio.l: And preuyly , he caugút hire , by the queynt
And seide I wysse , but icú haue my wylle
For derne loue , of the le~man , I spille
And held hire harde , by the haunche bones
And seide lemman , loue me al at ones
Or I wol deyen , also god me saue
And she sprong , as a colt doth in a traue
And with hire hed , she wryed fast a wey
She seyde , I wol nat kysse the , be my fey
We lat be q¶ icú , lat be Nicholas
Or I wol crie , ou©t herrowe , and allas
Do wey ©oure handes , for ©oure curteisie
¢ This Nicholas , gan mäcy for to crye
And spak so faire , and profred hi~ so faste
That she hire loue , hym gunted at the laste
And swor hire otú , by Seynt Thoms of kent
That she wolde ben , at his comaundement
Whan æat she may , hire leiser wel a spie
Myn husbond is , so ful of Ielusye
That but ©e wayte wel , and be preue
I wot rigút wel , I nam but ded q¶ she
ñe must be ful derne , as in this cas
¢ Nay ther of care the nougút , q¶ Nicholas
A Clerk had litherly , byset his whyle
But if he coude , a Carpenter begyle
And thus they ben , a corde¶ , and I sworn~
To wayte a tyme , as I haue told byforn~
Whan Nicholas had don this , euäydele

note.notetxt: ¢ Nota bene117 The words are written in the left marginEst

folio.l: And thakkede hire , a boute the lendes wele
He kisseth hire swete , and taketh his sautrie
And pleyeth faste , and maketh melodye
¢ Tha~ne fel it thus , æ to the parissú Chirche
Cristes owen werk , for to werche
This good wyf went , on an haliday
Hire forhede shonö , as brigút as ony day
So was it wasshen , whan she lete hir werk
¢ Now was there of æ chirche , a parissú clerk
The which , æat was I cleped , Absolouö
Crool was his heer , & as the gold it shonö
And strotted as a fanne , large and brode
Ful streyt and euene , lay his ioly shode
His rode was rede , his eyen grey as goos
With Poules wyndowes , coruen on hise shoos
